recommendation.title: Full rest — HRV down, TSB still negative
recommendation.reasoning: Friday isn't in the plan at all — it's a natural rest day between Thursday's tempo and Saturday's long run — and today's numbers back that up. HRV came in at 65ms, 17% below the 78ms baseline, on its own landing in the 10-20% 'reduced intensity' band. TSB is -19.2, past the -15 caution threshold but nowhere near the -25 swap-to-easy line, and sleep score was a solid 80 (7.7h). Nothing here clears the swap-to-easy bar on its own, but together they confirm rest is the right call, not just a calendar gap to fill.
recommendation.sessionDetail: No workout scheduled. Full rest — no run, no swim, no strength work. Let TSB climb back toward neutral before Saturday's 55-minute long run.
recommendation.bikeNote: Bike both ways is fine — it's a scheduled rest day and an easy commute won't add meaningful load, but keep it easy, no hard efforts or hill pushes with TSB still at -19.2.

sessions.lesson: Avg HR stayed at 139 (inside the easy ceiling) for the whole first MP session, though a max spike to 176 during the intervals is sharper than a pure 'familiarity' session should produce.
Pace (5.91/km) ran slower than the 5:00-5:15/km target, yet avg HR still sat at 139 — the top of the easy zone — showing the week's fatigue made even a slow pace cost more effort than it should.
Swim class stayed exactly at the low, aerobic effort it called for (avg HR 111) — solid active recovery, and the last class before the Monday slot ends.
Unscheduled Sunday session with 0km raw GPS and a moderate 116bpm average — this is the apartment-move exertion (second straight day), not a training session.
Distance and duration overran the 8km/45min prescription by about 55%, and avg HR 154 ran well into tempo effort rather than easy pace — the single biggest driver of that week's deep-negative TSB.

recentActivity.analysis: TSS has settled back into base-phase norms (32-36) after Jul 11's long-run overrun spiked to 76.8, with the move exertion (Jul 12) and swim class (Jul 13) both landing in a similar mid-30s band. HR control looks better than the week before: Tuesday's and today's runs both averaged 139bpm, right at the top of the easy zone rather than crossing into it like Jul 11's 154bpm long run did — the fatigue signature is easing but hasn't fully cleared.
